justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified as a domain model change — Bird opens by stating this couples two previously independent bounded contexts and changes ordering from optimistic to pessimistic. (2) Data ownership question raised — Bird asks who owns 'available stock' and defines it as a derived concept distinct from StockLevel. (3) TOCTOU/simultaneous-order race condition identified — AC-4 directly addresses concurrent orders, and the analysis explains why a raw stock check is insufficient. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — Bird explicitly states 'Stock Reservation does not currently exist in either context and must be introduced' and separates the advisory read-model from the authoritative write-side check. (5) Escalation to MJ — Bird flags architectural decisions (ACL, event-based integration, fail-open vs fail-closed) and marks them as requiring architectural resolution. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-2 explicitly defines the invariant that an Order must not be confirmed if stock is insufficient.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough separation of the advisory indicator (read-model, eventually consistent) from the blocking check (write-side, strongly consistent) — this goes beyond the rubric requirement
Identified phantom reservation risk (leaked reservations silently reducing sellable inventory) — a subtle failure mode not in the rubric
Correctly identified that dependency direction must be Sales → Warehouse, never the reverse, codified as BR-5
Defined reservation TTL/expiry as a business rule (BR-6) and acceptance criterion (AC-6), addressing the reservation lifecycle
AC-5 explicitly flags fail-open vs fail-closed as a business decision, not a technical one — exactly the kind of undefined question Bird should surface
Critical Domain Warning #1 directly addresses the naive 'just add a stock check' anti-pattern from the failure modes
MJ escalation is implicit rather than explicit — Bird flags architectural decisions but does not use a direct 'escalate to MJ' callout with a structured handoff

agent_output_excerpt:
## Domain Analysis — Cross-Context Stock Availability Integration

---

### Domain Analysis

**Business Context:**
This feature request asks us to couple two previously independent bounded contexts — Warehouse Management (stock truth) and Sales/Order Management (order lifecycle) — by introducing a real-time stock check at order placement time and a live availability indicator in the sales UI. This is a significant domain integration that changes the fundamental nature of the ordering process fro
